systematic budget into the chains at the aggregation step, toggled by the

than a refit. The budget is anchored to the DESI companion modelling result that

, dominated by the wiggle/no-wiggle
split, FoG placement, and template-dilation choices catalogued in the decision
register. Fiducial-cosmology dependence is swept through

(a set of AbacusSummit grids), and the
remaining

decisions exist for sensitivity tests; none
moves the baseline result beyond its quoted budget.

precision in the strongest LRG bins, and yields a self-consistent set of

The article’s claims hyperlink into this register of ASTRA objects: the findings
that back each result, the decisions that configured the pipeline, and the

each inline

reference and each decision option carries its

The BAO feature in ξ(s) visibly sharpens from pre- to post-reconstruction across tracers.

The isolated-feature curves show a narrower, taller peak near
100 Mpc/h post-recon than pre-recon, consistent with
displacement-field reconstruction undoing the non-linear bulk
flows that smear the acoustic signature.
The acoustic feature is detected post-reconstruction in every tracer.

Δχ²(α_iso) profiles comparing the damped-BAO template to a
no-BAO reference show a well-defined minimum near α_iso ≈ 1
in each case.
Scope: LRG1, LRG2, LRG3, ELG1, ELG2, LRG3+ELG1 — post-reconstruction, baseline universe.
Reconstruction shrinks σ(α_iso) across every fitted tracer.

σ(α_iso) falls by a factor of ~1.4–1.8 across the 2D-fit tracers
(LRG1 0.0167 → 0.0114, LRG2 0.0198 → 0.0113, LRG3 0.0131 →
0.0093) and by ~2.9× for the 1D ELG1 fit (0.0772 → 0.0266).
Scope: LRG1/LRG2/LRG3/ELG1 under the baseline universe.
Post-recon α_iso is measured at roughly 1 % fractional precision per 2D LRG bin.

σ(α_iso)/α_iso = 1.17 % for LRG1, 1.18 % for LRG2, and 0.93 %
for LRG3 — placing the BAO scale at these redshifts to
sub-percent in the best case.
Scope: LRG1/LRG2/LRG3 post-recon iso_AP fits, baseline universe.
Post-recon fits return χ²/dof consistent with the number of degrees of freedom.

Per-tracer values: LRG1 38.1/39, LRG2 36.5/39, LRG3 27.5/39,
ELG1 20.4/19. The damped-BAO template plus spline broadband
adequately describes the measured correlation functions over
the fit range.
Scope: Post-recon iso_AP fits, baseline universe.

The ξ covariance for every BAO fit comes from RascalC
(semi-analytic 4-point integral + jackknife shot-noise rescaling).
RascalC is calibrated against the DESI fiducial reconstruction +
clustering pipeline: IterativeFFT RecSym, Σ_sm=15, lin4 s-binning,
WEIGHT*WEIGHT_FKP weights, and the specific random density used in
the published runs. Choosing the published VAC cov therefore locks
downstream recon + clustering decisions to the fiducial options;
the

alternative was considered
(misharash/RascalC-scripts + Misha’s RascalC C++ fork on NERSC)
but is excluded as out of scope for this reproduction -- see

Ingested from /global/cfs/cdirs/desi/users/mrash/RascalC/Y1/
unblinded/v1.2/. One cov per tracer × pre/post state.

Full RascalC pipeline (pair counting + 4-point integration +
jackknife rescaling) against DR1 catalogs, per
misharash/RascalC-scripts.

~20 node-days of compute across all tracers × pre/post, plus
a private

(cosmodesi/desi-y1-kp) dependency
for filename resolution, and a version-drift issue (scripts
target v1.5, published cov is v1.2). Not in scope for this
reproduction. Choosing this option would also require
unlocking non-fiducial recon + clustering decisions (not

Smoothing scale Sigma_sm applied to the density field before solving
for the displacement, for the BGS / LRG / ELG parents.
The BAO model’s damping correction uses the same value, so recon and
fit must agree -- that’s why this lives at the root and recon inherits
it. QSO has a distinct fiducial (sm30 vs sm15 here) and is
parameterized separately via

QSO’s fiducial reconstruction smoothing is 30 h⁻¹ Mpc (vs 15 h⁻¹ Mpc
for BGS/LRG/ELG), reflecting the QSO sample’s lower number density
and larger coherence scale. The BAO model’s damping template must
match, so both the recon script and the fit scripts branch on
to consume this decision rather than
. sm20 is the sensitivity-test alternative for QSO.

Spline-based broadband (fiducial) vs polynomial-based (legacy form).
They give consistent BAO scales; the small difference goes into the
systematic error budget.

DESI adopts a cubic-spline-based broadband model as fiducial, replacing the polynomial broadband used by previous (e)BOSS analyses, because the spline method has stronger theoretical motivation and is less arbitrary in its choice of free parameters.

Gaussian priors on Σ_perp, Σ_par, Σ_s centered at fiducial values.
Gaussian is more robust than flat priors, but results are consistent
either way.

Tight Gaussian priors (width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc) on the BAO damping parameters centered on theory values are recommended; fully fixing them risks biases and flat priors can also bias α and weaken constraints on noisy data.

Biases of order 0.1% in α_iso and 0.2% in α_AP appear when the damping parameters are misspecified by more than ~2 h^-1 Mpc from their fiducial values, justifying Gaussian priors of width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc.

Σ⊥ and Σ∥ follow Σ⊥ = Σ0·G, Σ∥ = Σ0·G·(1+f) where Σ0 = 12.4 h^-1 Mpc for σ8 = 0.9 at z=0, scaling linearly with σ8 and redshift via the growth function -- the theoretical prediction that anchors the Gaussian prior centres.

Nonlinear erasure of the BAO is modelled as convolution with a Gaussian Lagrangian-displacement distribution, which Fourier-transforms to the multiplicative exp(-k²Σ²/2) damping form used in BAO template fits.

Because Lagrangian displacements are dominated by bulk flows, galaxy bias has only a small effect on Σ⊥/Σ∥: highly biased tracers increase Lagrangian variances only by small amounts. Supports tracer-common Σ priors with modest tracer-dependent offsets.
Uniform priors U[0, 20] for each damping parameter.

Tight Gaussian priors (width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc) on the BAO damping parameters centered on theory values are recommended; fully fixing them risks biases and flat priors can also bias α and weaken constraints on noisy data.

Fiducial Σ_perp, Σ_par, Σ_s per tracer × recon state.
The per-tracer centers live in

Unscaled fiducial centers for the tracer being fit.

Σ⊥ and Σ∥ follow Σ⊥ = Σ0·G, Σ∥ = Σ0·G·(1+f) where Σ0 = 12.4 h^-1 Mpc for σ8 = 0.9 at z=0, scaling linearly with σ8 and redshift via the growth function -- the theoretical prediction that anchors the Gaussian prior centres.

Because Lagrangian displacements are dominated by bulk flows, galaxy bias has only a small effect on Σ⊥/Σ∥: highly biased tracers increase Lagrangian variances only by small amounts. Supports tracer-common Σ priors with modest tracer-dependent offsets.

Fiducial uses the peak-average method of Brieden, Gil-Marín & Verde
2022 to split P_linear into wiggle + smooth components.
Eisenstein-Hu 1998 and Savitzky-Golay log-space filters are the
historical alternatives. The systematic budget assigns < 0.02% α_iso
/ < 0.09% α_AP to this choice. Only the fiducial is wired.
The DESI fiducial BAO template is constructed using the Wallisch (2018) peak-averaging/dewiggling method; previous BOSS/eBOSS analyses used varying methods (Eisenstein-Hu, Kirkby, etc.).

The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred.

The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred.

Σ_s multiplies the smooth (no-wiggle) component only; the legacy
approach applied it to the full template, which introduces a
degeneracy with the BAO damping. “smooth_only” is the fiducial,
built into desilike’s DampedBAOWigglesTracer... model;
the “full_model” alternative is declared but not wired
(would need a custom theory wrapper).
Σ_s multiplies P_nw only; desilike default.

The fiducial DESI choice applies the Fingers-of-God (Σ_s) damping only to the smooth model component and not to the BAO wiggle component; previous models applied FoG to both, and the fiducial decoupling makes BAO damping interpretation simpler.
Including the FoG damping only in the smooth component removes a clear degeneracy between the BAO (Σ_||) damping and the FoG (Σ_s) damping scales, making prior enforcement and convergence easier.
Σ_s multiplies wiggle + smooth; not wired, raises NotImplementedError.

Placing the FoG damping on the wiggle component in addition to the smooth component shifts α_AP by an average of 0.11% (a 2σ-detected systematic) and α_iso by ≤ 0.02%, motivating inclusion of this effect in the modelling systematic budget.

The α_iso / α_AP rescaling is applied to the wiggle component only;
the smooth P_nw is evaluated at observed coordinates without dilation.
Dilating the full template is the alternative; the systematic budget
assigns < 0.02% α_iso / < 0.09% α_AP to this choice. Only the
fiducial is wired.
α rescales P_w; P_nw at observed coords. desilike default.

DESI fiducial practice dilates only the BAO wiggle component by α, not the full template; previous analyses dilated both smooth and wiggle components, and dilating only wiggles guarantees that α captures only BAO information.
α rescales wiggle + smooth; not wired, raises NotImplementedError.
Whether the smooth component is dilated in addition to the wiggle component induces a modelling systematic of < 0.02% on α_iso and < 0.09% on α_AP.

AbacusSummit cosmologies used to test fiducial-cosmology systematics.
c000 is Planck-2018 ΛCDM (fiducial).
Planck 2018 TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing base-ΛCDM gives Ω_b h² = 0.02237, Ω_c h² = 0.1200, n_s = 0.9649, H0 = 67.36 km/s/Mpc, Ω_m = 0.3153 -- the values adopted as AbacusSummit c000.
Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM constrains the sound horizon at the drag epoch to r_drag = 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc, the CMB anchor for the BAO standard ruler.
Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM assumes three neutrino species approximated as two massless and a single massive neutrino of mass m_ν = 0.06 eV; Ω_m includes this neutrino contribution.
Planck 2018 + BAO constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with the Standard Model N_eff = 3.046 -- showing AbacusSummit c003 (N_eff = 3.70) is well outside the Planck preferred range.
Planck 2018 + BAO constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with the Standard Model N_eff = 3.046 -- showing AbacusSummit c003 (N_eff = 3.70) is well outside the Planck preferred range.

Combined modelling systematic (0.1% α_iso, 0.2% α_AP, conservatively
0.2% on α_⊥ / α_∥) added in quadrature to the reported σ. Consumed
only by make_alpha_table.py -- the MCMC chains themselves are
untouched, so both options can be produced from the same fits by
re-running the table step. The stat-only σ is always kept as a
companion column (
Summing theory and modelling systematics in quadrature, the recommended combined systematic budget for DESI post-reconstruction BAO is 0.1% on α_iso and 0.2% on α_AP.

Placing the FoG damping on the wiggle component in addition to the smooth component shifts α_AP by an average of 0.11% (a 2σ-detected systematic) and α_iso by ≤ 0.02%, motivating inclusion of this effect in the modelling systematic budget.

The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred.
Whether the smooth component is dilated in addition to the wiggle component induces a modelling systematic of < 0.02% on α_iso and < 0.09% on α_AP.

covariance_source.label: Covariance matrix source
covariance_source.rationale:
The ξ covariance for every BAO fit comes from RascalC
(semi-analytic 4-point integral + jackknife shot-noise rescaling).
RascalC is calibrated against the DESI fiducial reconstruction +
clustering pipeline: IterativeFFT RecSym, Σ_sm=15, lin4 s-binning,
WEIGHT*WEIGHT_FKP weights, and the specific random density used in
the published runs. Choosing the published VAC cov therefore locks
downstream recon + clustering decisions to the fiducial options;
the `self_computed` alternative was considered
(misharash/RascalC-scripts + Misha's RascalC C++ fork on NERSC)
but is excluded as out of scope for this reproduction -- see
`excluded_reason`.

smoothing_radius.label: Reconstruction Gaussian smoothing scale (BGS/LRG/ELG)
smoothing_radius.rationale:
Smoothing scale Sigma_sm applied to the density field before solving
for the displacement, for the BGS / LRG / ELG parents.
The BAO model's damping correction uses the same value, so recon and
fit must agree -- that's why this lives at the root and recon inherits
it. QSO has a distinct fiducial (sm30 vs sm15 here) and is
parameterized separately via `smoothing_radius_qso`.

smoothing_radius_qso.label: Reconstruction Gaussian smoothing scale (QSO only)
smoothing_radius_qso.rationale:
QSO's fiducial reconstruction smoothing is 30 h⁻¹ Mpc (vs 15 h⁻¹ Mpc
for BGS/LRG/ELG), reflecting the QSO sample's lower number density
and larger coherence scale. The BAO model's damping template must
match, so both the recon script and the fit scripts branch on
`tracer.id == "qso"` to consume this decision rather than
`smoothing_radius`. sm20 is the sensitivity-test alternative for QSO.

broadband.label: Broadband model
broadband.rationale:
Spline-based broadband (fiducial) vs polynomial-based (legacy form).
They give consistent BAO scales; the small difference goes into the
systematic error budget.

damping_prior.label: BAO damping parameter prior
damping_prior.rationale:
Gaussian priors on Σ_perp, Σ_par, Σ_s centered at fiducial values.
Gaussian is more robust than flat priors, but results are consistent
either way.

fit_range.label: Fit range in s
fit_range.rationale:
Fiducial is s ∈ [48, 152] Mpc/h at 4 Mpc/h binning.
Extend/narrow as sensitivity tests.

ells.label: Multipoles fit (LRG only; 1D tracers override)
ells.rationale:
LRG bins are fit with 2D (ℓ=0, 2). ELG1 / QSO are 1D-only regardless
of this setting -- the tracer's `ells` in `scripts/tracers.py` caps
the universe choice via an intersection in
`bao_utils.resolve_settings`, so `twod` means "2D where supported".

damping_centers.label: Damping prior central values
damping_centers.rationale:
Fiducial Σ_perp, Σ_par, Σ_s per tracer × recon state.
The per-tracer centers live in `scripts/tracers.py`; this decision is the
multiplicative scaling applied to them. Variants widen / narrow the
centers as a systematic test.

fitting_method.label: BAO fit method
fitting_method.rationale:
Fiducial reports posterior-based intervals for the combined-tracer
rows and headline cosmological parameters — those come from emcee
chains. Profiling (Minuit) gives a faster point estimate +
parabolic errors, useful as a pipeline sanity check but not the
fiducial for reported alphas.

template_cosmology.label: BAO template cosmology
template_cosmology.rationale:
AbacusSummit cosmologies used to test fiducial-cosmology systematics.
c000 is Planck-2018 ΛCDM (fiducial).

fog_placement.label: FoG damping placement
fog_placement.rationale:
Σ_s multiplies the smooth (no-wiggle) component only; the legacy
approach applied it to the full template, which introduces a
degeneracy with the BAO damping. "smooth_only" is the fiducial,
built into desilike's DampedBAOWigglesTracer... model;
the "full_model" alternative is declared but not wired
(would need a custom theory wrapper).

dewiggling_method.label: Wiggle / no-wiggle split
dewiggling_method.rationale:
Fiducial uses the peak-average method of Brieden, Gil-Marín & Verde
2022 to split P_linear into wiggle + smooth components.
Eisenstein-Hu 1998 and Savitzky-Golay log-space filters are the
historical alternatives. The systematic budget assigns < 0.02% α_iso
/ < 0.09% α_AP to this choice. Only the fiducial is wired.

dilate_model_component.label: Component that α dilates
dilate_model_component.rationale:
The α_iso / α_AP rescaling is applied to the wiggle component only;
the smooth P_nw is evaluated at observed coordinates without dilation.
Dilating the full template is the alternative; the systematic budget
assigns < 0.02% α_iso / < 0.09% α_AP to this choice. Only the
fiducial is wired.

systematic_error_treatment.label: Reported error includes modelling systematic
systematic_error_treatment.rationale:
Combined modelling systematic (0.1% α_iso, 0.2% α_AP, conservatively
0.2% on α_⊥ / α_∥) added in quadrature to the reported σ. Consumed
only by make_alpha_table.py -- the MCMC chains themselves are
untouched, so both options can be produced from the same fits by
re-running the table step. The stat-only σ is always kept as a
companion column (`alpha{1,2}_std_stat`).

bao_peak_sharpens_post_recon.label: BAO peak sharpens
bao_peak_sharpens_post_recon.claim: The BAO feature in ξ(s) visibly sharpens from pre- to post-reconstruction across tracers.
bao_peak_sharpens_post_recon.notes:
The isolated-feature curves show a narrower, taller peak near
100 Mpc/h post-recon than pre-recon, consistent with
displacement-field reconstruction undoing the non-linear bulk
flows that smear the acoustic signature.

bao_detected_post_recon.label: BAO detected post-recon
bao_detected_post_recon.claim: The acoustic feature is detected post-reconstruction in every tracer.
bao_detected_post_recon.notes:
Δχ²(α_iso) profiles comparing the damped-BAO template to a
no-BAO reference show a well-defined minimum near α_iso ≈ 1
in each case.
bao_detected_post_recon.scope: LRG1, LRG2, LRG3, ELG1, ELG2, LRG3+ELG1 — post-reconstruction, baseline universe.

recon_reduces_alpha_iso_sigma.label: Reconstruction tightens α_iso
recon_reduces_alpha_iso_sigma.claim: Reconstruction shrinks σ(α_iso) across every fitted tracer.
recon_reduces_alpha_iso_sigma.notes:
σ(α_iso) falls by a factor of ~1.4–1.8 across the 2D-fit tracers
(LRG1 0.0167 → 0.0114, LRG2 0.0198 → 0.0113, LRG3 0.0131 →
0.0093) and by ~2.9× for the 1D ELG1 fit (0.0772 → 0.0266).

subpercent_alpha_iso_precision.label: ~1 % α_iso precision
subpercent_alpha_iso_precision.claim: Post-recon α_iso is measured at roughly 1 % fractional precision per 2D LRG bin.
subpercent_alpha_iso_precision.notes:
σ(α_iso)/α_iso = 1.17 % for LRG1, 1.18 % for LRG2, and 0.93 %
for LRG3 — placing the BAO scale at these redshifts to
sub-percent in the best case.
subpercent_alpha_iso_precision.scope: LRG1/LRG2/LRG3 post-recon iso_AP fits, baseline universe.

bao_fit_chi2_near_dof.label: χ²/dof near dof
bao_fit_chi2_near_dof.claim: Post-recon fits return χ²/dof consistent with the number of degrees of freedom.
bao_fit_chi2_near_dof.notes:
Per-tracer values: LRG1 38.1/39, LRG2 36.5/39, LRG3 27.5/39,
ELG1 20.4/19. The damped-BAO template plus spline broadband
adequately describes the measured correlation functions over
the fit range.

fog_decoupled_from_wiggles_fiducial.claim: The fiducial DESI choice applies the Fingers-of-God (Σ_s) damping only to the smooth model component and not to the BAO wiggle component; previous models applied FoG to both, and the fiducial decoupling makes BAO damping interpretation simpler.
fog_decoupled_from_wiggles_fiducial.quote: We apply the Finger-of-God damping to only the smooth model components, allowing for no interaction with the BAO wiggles. This makes interpreting the impact of the BAO damping parameters simpler. Previous models applied the FoG term equally to both the wiggle and no-wiggle components.

fog_decoupling_breaks_degeneracy.claim: Including the FoG damping only in the smooth component removes a clear degeneracy between the BAO (Σ_||) damping and the FoG (Σ_s) damping scales, making prior enforcement and convergence easier.
fog_decoupling_breaks_degeneracy.quote: Including the FoG damping in only the smooth model component is preferable as it removes a clear degeneracy between the BAO and FoG damping scales.

fog_placement_systematic_size.claim: Placing the FoG damping on the wiggle component in addition to the smooth component shifts α_AP by an average of 0.11% (a 2σ-detected systematic) and α_iso by ≤ 0.02%, motivating inclusion of this effect in the modelling systematic budget.
fog_placement_systematic_size.quote: the largest average difference is ≤ 0.02% and 0.11% for αiso and αap respectively. Albeit small, this latter difference is detected with 2σ significance and so should be accounted for in our final systematic error budget.

wallisch_dewiggling_fiducial.claim: The DESI fiducial BAO template is constructed using the Wallisch (2018) peak-averaging/dewiggling method; previous BOSS/eBOSS analyses used varying methods (Eisenstein-Hu, Kirkby, etc.).
wallisch_dewiggling_fiducial.quote: The DESI BAO template is constructed following the method of Wallisch (2018) Previous BOSS and eBOSS analyses have used varying methods for extracting the template

dewiggling_method_systematic_size.claim: The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred.
dewiggling_method_systematic_size.quote: Our tests do not point to a single correct choice, but we find a maximum systematic error associated with this choice of only 0.02% and 0.09% for αiso and αap respectively when following our fiducial fitting methodology.

dilate_wiggles_only_fiducial.claim: DESI fiducial practice dilates only the BAO wiggle component by α, not the full template; previous analyses dilated both smooth and wiggle components, and dilating only wiggles guarantees that α captures only BAO information.
dilate_wiggles_only_fiducial.quote: We dilate only the BAO wiggles by the α’s. Previous analyses generally dilated both the smooth and wiggle components of the model. We believe the new method to be preferable as it guarantees only information from the BAO is captured.

dilate_wiggles_only_systematic_size.claim: Whether the smooth component is dilated in addition to the wiggle component induces a modelling systematic of < 0.02% on α_iso and < 0.09% on α_AP.
dilate_wiggles_only_systematic_size.quote: we find that the choice of whether or not to dilate the smooth model component in addition to the wiggles results in a systematic error of < 0.02% (< 0.09%) for αiso (αap).

spline_broadband_fiducial.claim: DESI adopts a cubic-spline-based broadband model as fiducial, replacing the polynomial broadband used by previous (e)BOSS analyses, because the spline method has stronger theoretical motivation and is less arbitrary in its choice of free parameters.
spline_broadband_fiducial.quote: We use the spline-based broadband model validated in §7.1. Previous analyses used polynomials (of slightly varying degrees and functional forms) to fit the broadband. We find the spline method has better theoretical motivation, and to be less arbitrary in its choice of free parameters.

damping_prior_gaussian_fiducial.claim: Tight Gaussian priors (width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc) on the BAO damping parameters centered on theory values are recommended; fully fixing them risks biases and flat priors can also bias α and weaken constraints on noisy data.
damping_prior_gaussian_fiducial.quote: We recommend choosing the BAO damping parameters based on theoretical calculations and allowing these to vary within reasonably tight priors

damping_prior_width_justification.claim: Biases of order 0.1% in α_iso and 0.2% in α_AP appear when the damping parameters are misspecified by more than ~2 h^-1 Mpc from their fiducial values, justifying Gaussian priors of width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc.
damping_prior_width_justification.quote: biases of order 0.1% and 0.2% for αiso and αap respectively occur when the Σ values are changed by more than ∼ 2h−1 Mpc from their fiducial values.

combined_systematic_budget.claim: Summing theory and modelling systematics in quadrature, the recommended combined systematic budget for DESI post-reconstruction BAO is 0.1% on α_iso and 0.2% on α_AP.
combined_systematic_budget.quote: Combining our two prongs of investigation we find that the combined systematics in BAO fitting can be controlled, conservatively, to within 0.1% for αiso and 0.2% for αap.

sigma_perp_parallel_formula.scope: Theoretical prediction of BAO damping scales
sigma_perp_parallel_formula.claim: Σ⊥ and Σ∥ follow Σ⊥ = Σ0·G, Σ∥ = Σ0·G·(1+f) where Σ0 = 12.4 h^-1 Mpc for σ8 = 0.9 at z=0, scaling linearly with σ8 and redshift via the growth function -- the theoretical prediction that anchors the Gaussian prior centres.
sigma_perp_parallel_formula.quote: The rms radial displacements across (Σ⊥) and along the line of sight (Σk) at this separation follow Σ⊥ = Σ0G and Σk = Σ0G(1 + f), where Σ0 = 12.4h−1 Mpc for a cosmology with σ8 = 0.9 at the present-day, f = d(ln G)/d(ln a) ∼ Ω0.6m , and G is the growth function while G is normalized to be G = 0.758 at z = 0 such that G(z) = (1 + z)−1 at high z.

gaussian_lagrangian_displacement_model.claim: Nonlinear erasure of the BAO is modelled as convolution with a Gaussian Lagrangian-displacement distribution, which Fourier-transforms to the multiplicative exp(-k²Σ²/2) damping form used in BAO template fits.
gaussian_lagrangian_displacement_model.quote: A broadened peak will have the same power spectrum multiplied with an additional damping envelope set by the Fourier transformation of the broadened peak shape. For example, if the peak in the correlation function is broadened with a Gaussian function due to the Silk damping effect (Σs) and Lagrangian displacement distribution (Σnl), then the convolution in configuration space with the Gaussian function is Fourier-transformed to a multiplicative exponential factor in Fourier space.

bias_weak_effect_on_sigma.claim: Because Lagrangian displacements are dominated by bulk flows, galaxy bias has only a small effect on Σ⊥/Σ∥: highly biased tracers increase Lagrangian variances only by small amounts. Supports tracer-common Σ priors with modest tracer-dependent offsets.
bias_weak_effect_on_sigma.quote: From Eisenstein et al. (2006), the Lagrangian displacement fields due to nonlinear growth and nonlinear redshift distortions at separation of 100h−1 Mpc can be approximated as an elliptical Gaussian function. Since most of the effect comes from the bulk flow, galaxy bias does not have much effect on Lagrangian displacement.

planck2018_headline_parameters.claim: Planck 2018 TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing base-ΛCDM gives Ω_b h² = 0.02237, Ω_c h² = 0.1200, n_s = 0.9649, H0 = 67.36 km/s/Mpc, Ω_m = 0.3153 -- the values adopted as AbacusSummit c000.

planck2018_rdrag_precision.claim: Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM constrains the sound horizon at the drag epoch to r_drag = 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc, the CMB anchor for the BAO standard ruler.

planck2018_neutrino_convention.claim: Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM assumes three neutrino species approximated as two massless and a single massive neutrino of mass m_ν = 0.06 eV; Ω_m includes this neutrino contribution.
planck2018_neutrino_convention.quote: We assume three neutrinos species, approximated as two massless states and a single massive neutrino of mass mν = 0.06 eV.

planck2018_neff_constraint.claim: Planck 2018 + BAO constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with the Standard Model N_eff = 3.046 -- showing AbacusSummit c003 (N_eff = 3.70) is well outside the Planck preferred range.
planck2018_neff_constraint.quote: Combining with baryon acoustic oscillation (BAO) measurements (and considering single-parameter extensions) we constrain the eﬀective extra relativistic degrees of freedom to beNeﬀ = 2.99±0.17, in agreement with the Standard Model prediction Neﬀ = 3.046, and ﬁnd that the neutrino mass is tightly constrained to ∑mν < 0.12 eV.

recon_sharpens_bao_peak.claim: Density-field reconstruction sharpens the acoustic peak in the correlation function (equivalently, restores higher harmonics in the power spectrum) -- the core motivation for applying reconstruction before BAO measurement.
recon_sharpens_bao_peak.quote: By suitable reconstruction of the linear density field, one can sharpen the acoustic peak in the correlation function or, equivalently, restore the higher harmonics of the oscillations in the power spectrum.

nonlinear_peak_smearing.claim: Nonlinear structure formation and redshift-space distortions displace galaxies from their initial positions, blurring the BAO peak at ~150 Mpc and erasing higher harmonics in P(k).
nonlinear_peak_smearing.quote: Non-linear gravitational structure formation and redshift distortions move galaxies away from their original locations, blurring out the peak at 150 Mpc separation, or, equivalently, erasing the higher harmonics in the power spectrum that represent smaller scales

precision_loss_factor_three.claim: Nonlinear smearing reduces the precision of the BAO distance scale achievable per unit survey volume by roughly a factor of three at the present day.
precision_loss_factor_three.quote: these nonlinear effects reduce the precision of the distance scale that can be measured from a given volume of space, by roughly a factor of three at the present day.

recon_effective_at_low_z.claim: Reconstruction is particularly effective at low redshift where nonlinearities are worst -- exactly the regime most sensitive to dark energy.
recon_effective_at_low_z.quote: Reconstruction is particularly effective at low redshift, where the non-linearities are worse but where the dark energy density is highest.

factor_of_two_gain_at_z03.claim: At z=0.3, reconstruction can reduce the sample-variance error bar on the acoustic scale by at least a factor of 2 and in principle nearly a factor of 4.
factor_of_two_gain_at_z03.quote: At z = 0.3, we find that one can reduce the sample variance error bar on the acoustic scale by at least a factor of 2 and in principle by nearly a factor of 4.

recon_factor_two_demonstrated.claim: The simple linear-theory reconstruction in this paper improves distance precision from 1.4%/1.9% to 0.75%/0.95% in real/redshift space at z=0.3 -- the canonical factor-of-two gain.
recon_factor_two_demonstrated.quote: The simple reconstruction presented in this section improves these measurements to 0.75% and 0.95% in real and redshift space, respectively. This is a factor of two improvement.

move_densities_not_particles.claim: The conceptual basis of reconstruction is to move measured densities (not all particles) back to their initial location -- established by shifting a reference grid of smoothly distributed particles alongside the real particles and differencing the densities.
move_densities_not_particles.quote: Note that, in contrast to the work of Sirko & Spergel (2006), the point is not to move all of the particles back to their initial location, but rather to move the measured densities back to their initial location.

reference_grid_shifted_same.claim: The seminal recon algorithm shifts a reference grid of smoothly distributed particles (i.e., randoms) by the same displacement field as the real particles -- the operational prescription behind RecSym.
reference_grid_shifted_same.quote: We do the same for a reference grid of smoothly distributed particles.

reconstructed_density_as_difference.claim: The reconstructed density field is defined as the difference between the shifted real-particle density and the shifted reference-particle density -- the symmetric convention that displaces both tracers and randoms.
reconstructed_density_as_difference.quote: A new density field is defined by the difference of the density field of the real particles and that of the reference particles.

recon_easier_at_high_z.claim: Reconstruction is easier at higher redshifts because raw displacements are smaller there -- corollary: reconstruction is most needed, and provides the largest gains, at low redshift.
recon_easier_at_high_z.quote: Thus, reconstruction is easier at higher redshifts: the raw displacements are smaller, so one can accomplish sufficient reconstruction using only larger scales.

recon_improves_bao_by_2_to_3.claim: Overall conclusion: density-field reconstruction offers a factor-of-2-to-3 improvement to the BAO method, particularly at lower redshifts.
recon_improves_bao_by_2_to_3.quote: density-field reconstruction offers a significant opportunity to improve the baryon acoustic peak method by a factor of 2 to 3, particularly at lower redshifts.

recon_reduces_bao_damping.claim: Reconstruction reduces the non-linear damping of the BAO oscillations by about a factor of two when the input density field is smoothed on the non-linear scale.
recon_reduces_bao_damping.quote: Reconstruction does reduce the damping of the oscillations, by about a factor of 2 when the input density field is smoothed on the non-linear scale.

recon_less_damped_pl.claim: Within the Lagrangian/Zel'dovich framework, the linear-power-spectrum contribution to the reconstructed power spectrum is less damped than in the non-linear power spectrum -- the core theoretical reason reconstruction sharpens the BAO peak.
recon_less_damped_pl.quote: This is the crux of the reconstruction method - that the PL contribution to the reconstructed power spectrum is less damped than in the nonlinear power spectrum.

recon_reduces_mode_coupling.claim: Reconstruction also reduces the mode-coupling terms that introduce out-of-phase oscillation components and shift the acoustic peak -- complementary to the damping-reduction motivation.
recon_reduces_mode_coupling.quote: Reconstruction also reduces the mode coupling terms which introduce an out of phase component of the oscillations or shift the peak.

reconstruction.name: Reconstruction
reconstruction.summary: Density-field reconstruction for DESI DR1 parent samples — one pass per parent (BGS 0.1–0.4, LRGs 0.4–1.1, ELGs 0.8–1.6, QSO 0.8–2.1), emitting parent-level shifted galaxy + random catalogs. Per-z-bin slicing is deferred to clustering, so the reconstruction solve sees the full parent redshift range; the shifted catalogs are then cut into tracer z-bins downstream at ξ-time.

clustering.name: Clustering
clustering.summary: Two-point correlation function ξ(s, μ) for DESI DR1 galaxy tracers. Per-tracer fan-out: xi_{pre,post}_recon_<tracer>. Composite tracers (LRG3+ELG1) union multiple parent NPZs and z-slice their catalogs at ξ time.
